summary: Tested 4 newly shipped features: public /status, promo-banner schedule, marketing-studio lifestyle endpoint validation, and bulletproof shield on 4 public endpoints. Backend: 12/12 new pytest cases pass + 11/11 existing resilience tests still pass. Frontend: /status renders perfectly with green 'All systems operational' banner, status-overall-label, status-trade-block, and 8 data-testid'd service rows. Marketing Studio admin renders schedule-toggle/card/start/end/save and lifestyle-card/toggle/search/room/variants/generate-btn. CRITICAL BUG FOUND: lifestyle tile-search → generate flow is broken because /api/tiles/search response has no `id` field; tile cards render with data-testid='marketing-lifestyle-tile-undefined' and clicking Generate would send tile_id=undefined.

minor.issue: Response objects expose only {name, slug, image, price, collection} — no `id` field. Marketing Studio lifestyle UI relies on `id` to wire selected tile into POST /api/admin/marketing-studio/lifestyle. Either add `id` to search response or have the frontend use `slug` and have lifestyle endpoint accept slug as a fallback.

ui_bugs.issue: data-testid renders as 'marketing-lifestyle-tile-undefined' for every result because t.id and t._id are both undefined on /api/tiles/search response. Also produces React 'unique key prop' console warning on the .map(). Use t.slug as the React key + testid, OR have the backend search return id.

integration_issues.issue: Picking a tile from the search results and clicking Generate would POST tile_id=undefined to /api/admin/marketing-studio/lifestyle, returning 422. The full lifestyle flow is therefore broken end-to-end via the search UI. Workaround: paste a real tile.id manually (not exposed in any UI). Fix: include `id` in /api/tiles/search response OR change lifestyle endpoint to accept `tile_slug` and update frontend.

action_items: Add `id` field to /api/tiles/search response objects (preferred) OR change MarketingStudio lifestyle UI to use t.slug + extend POST /lifestyle to accept tile_slug as alternative to tile_id
Use a stable React key (t.slug) on the lifestyle tile results .map() to silence the 'unique key prop' console warning

(Optional) Mention in lifestyle endpoint validation that lifestyle requires the tile to have a valid image — already covered by 422 'Tile has no image to use as reference', but worth surfacing in the admin UI when picked
critical_code_review_comments: marketing_studio.py PromoBannerUpdate uses req.dict() (Pydantic v2 deprecated; should be model_dump(exclude_unset=True)). Currently relies on `is not None` filtering which works.
public_status.py: response_ms is `last.get('elapsed_ms')` which may be None when health monitor stored under a different key — verified live data does include elapsed_ms, so OK.
public_status.py PUBLIC_LABELS map of 8 services is hard-coded. If health monitor adds/removes endpoints, this list will drift. Consider deriving from a single source of truth.
MarketingStudio.jsx ~line 634: tile result key/testid uses (t.id || t._id) but /api/tiles/search returns neither — see CRITICAL BUG above. This breaks lifestyle generation entirely from the UI.
_promo_active_now() correctly handles ISO datetimes with Z and timezone offsets; window logic is sound (manual_on OR in_window).
Bulletproof shield on the 4 public endpoints all return 200 healthy; verified via parametrised tests.

success_rate.frontend: 85% (UI present, but lifestyle search→generate integration broken)

seed_data_creation: None. Promo-banner state is restored after each schedule test via fixture teardown — promo-banner returns to its original (enabled=false) state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid. The lifestyle UI fix is small: either (a) include `id` in /api/tiles/search response, or (b) change MarketingStudio.jsx to use t.slug AND extend lifestyle endpoint to accept tile_slug. After the fix, retest the full search→pick→generate flow but DO NOT click Generate (it costs ~$0.04). Just verify the POST request body would have tile_id (or tile_slug) populated by inspecting the request via page.expect_request(...). Status page testing complete; promo-banner schedule flow complete and verified end-to-end (future window → enabled:false; active window → enabled:true).
rca of the issue: RCA: /api/tiles/search returns a slim public projection {name, slug, image, price, collection} without the document `id`. Marketing Studio lifestyle UI was developed against the assumption it would receive `id`; lines reading t.id || t._id resolve to undefined. Reproduction: login as admin → /admin/marketing-studio → click 'Lifestyle photo from a tile' toggle → search 'onyx' → 8 tile cards render but each has data-testid 'marketing-lifestyle-tile-undefined' and lifestyleTilePicked.id is undefined when one is clicked. Mitigation: smallest fix is to include `id` in the search response (1-line backend change) OR have the frontend store `slug` and have the lifestyle endpoint accept tile_slug as a lookup key.
